name: Pin ℂ to a specific object. There's no such thing as the bare abstract category — a category is a structure, not a unique thing, so ℂ/~ has no referent until ℂ is named. Make  compute the tuple from ℂ's data, not from a tuple you wrote by hand. Show the 17.28M addresses are realized, not merely available. 3³×4⁵×5⁴ is the size of the grid — the codomain. For Crystal = ℂ/~ you need the projection onto: every address inhabited by a real object of ℂ. Resolve the decode∘encode dilemma — the keystone. Fix only this and the centerpiece becomes a real theorem. Turn the folding/unfolding table into one proof. Force the value-counts, or name the ouroboros decoration.

phase_0.domain_name: Pin ℂ to a specific object. There's no such thing as the bare abstract category — a category is a structure, not a unique thing, so ℂ/~ has no referent until ℂ is named. Make  compute the tuple from ℂ's data, not from a tuple you wrote by hand. Show the 17.28M addresses are realized, not merely available. 3³×4⁵×5⁴ is the size of the grid — the codomain. For Crystal = ℂ/~ you need the projection onto: every address inhabited by a real object of ℂ. Resolve the decode∘encode dilemma — the keystone. Fix only this and the centerpiece becomes a real theorem. Turn the folding/unfolding table into one proof. Force the value-counts, or name the ouroboros decoration.
phase_0.boundary_condition: The set of all 17,280,000 crystal addresses forms a closed boundary: every object of the concrete category ℂ maps to exactly one address, and the projection is surjective onto the inhabited subset.

VINIT.chosen_element: void: the pre-categorical state before ℂ is chosen
VINIT.justification: No category, no objects, no addresses; initial void.

TANCH.chosen_element: the set of all crystal addresses (the codomain)
TANCH.justification: The terminal anchor is the complete address space; nothing outside it.

AFWD.justification: Forward morphism from the concrete category ℂ to the quotient.

AREV.chosen_element: section decode: Crystal → ℂ (chooses a canonical representative per fiber)
AREV.justification: Reverse morphism from the quotient back to a specific object in ℂ.

CLINK.justification: Chaining the projection and section; the round-trip is the identity on the quotient (encode∘decode = id_Crystal) and on the quotient of ℂ (decode∘encode = id_ℂ/~).

IMSCRIB.chosen_element: the identity object of ℂ (the terminal object) under the projection
IMSCRIB.justification: The terminal object maps to a self-referential address; it recognizes itself as the unique object with that address.

FSPLIT.chosen_element: the set of all crystal addresses (the whole codomain)
FSPLIT.justification: Co-multiplication: the entire set branches into the subset of inhabited addresses (those with a preimage in ℂ) and the subset of uninhabited addresses (no preimage).

FFUSE.chosen_element: the union of the inhabited and uninhabited subsets
FFUSE.justification: Multiplication: taking the union returns the original set of all addresses.

EVALT.chosen_element: inhabited address subset: addresses that have at least one object in ℂ mapping to them
EVALT.justification: True: the property that an address is realized is affirmed.

EVALF.chosen_element: uninhabited address subset: addresses with no object
EVALF.justification: False: the property that an address is not realized.

ENGAGR.chosen_element: the paradice: an address that is both inhabited and uninhabited (cannot happen in classical logic, but here it represents the dialetheia of the compression: the address exists but its fiber may be empty when considered from a different perspective)
ENGAGR.justification: In a paraconsistent setting, the address is simultaneously realized and unrealized across different contexts.

IFIX.chosen_element: the permanent record of the projection mapping: a static table mapping each object to its crystal address
IFIX.justification: Irreversible: once the category ℂ is fixed, the projection is determined; it cannot be changed.

phase_2.split_element: the set of all crystal addresses

phase_3.void_description: 00: The state before ℂ is chosen — no addresses, no objects.
phase_3.true_description: 01: An address is known to be inhabited (has a witness object in ℂ).
phase_3.false_description: 10: An address is known to be uninhabited (no object in ℂ maps to it).
phase_3.both_description: 11: An address that is both inhabited and uninhabited — a dialetheia. In the context of the compression, this can occur when the category ℂ is not fully determined, or when considering a different category.

steps.domain_action: Choose a concrete category ℂ (e.g., finite sets and functions) as the forward morphism into structure.
Define the address space of 27×1024×625 = 17,280,000 crystal addresses as the closed boundary.
Identify the terminal object of ℂ as the self-referential element that maps to its own address.
Split the entire address space into the inhabited subset and the uninhabited subset.
Affirm the inhabited subset: each address here has a witness object in ℂ.
Affirm the uninhabited subset: each address here has no preimage in ℂ.
Compose the split and evaluations to create a chain from all addresses to partitioned knowledge.
Enter the paradice: consider an address that is both inhabited and uninhabited (e.g., from two different categories) — held without resolution.
Decode: from each inhabited address, choose a canonical representative object in ℂ via the section decode.
Fuse the inhabited and uninhabited subsets back into the whole address set via union.
Permanently record the projection mapping as an append-only table: each object ℂ maps to its unique crystal address.
Close the system; the crystal is sealed as the quotient ℂ/~.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: The quotient map encode: ℂ → Crystal, which translates objects and morphisms into their 12-tuple addresses.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Addresses are shared between components; communication happens by referring to the address of an object's equivalence class.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The 17.28M address space, each stored as a 27×1024×625 mixed-radix number.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The 4-stage derivation (as_above_induction) that computes the order of promotion steps from ℂ to the crystal.
phase_5.alfs_store: The Lean-verified bijection decode∘encode = id, serving as the canonical reference store: any address can be decoded to a representative and re-encoded to the same address.

phase_6.cycle_cost: The cost is the effort of constructing ℂ and computing the projection; each cycle of encode/decode is idempotent.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: The raw category ℂ with its objects and morphisms.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: The crystal address space with inhabited and uninhabited addresses distinguished.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The compression is structurally lossless: the round-trip preserves the equivalence class, and the partition into inhabited/uninhabited is exact.

  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑙 𐑡)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Permanently record the projection mapping as an append-only table: each objec...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑭 𐑼)  -- [12] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Close the system; the crystal is sealed as the quotient ℂ/~.)
